records.ety: [Icel. gjörð girdle, or gerð girth; akin to Goth. garda girdle. See Gird to girt, and cf. Girdle, n.]

defs: A band or strap which encircles the body; especially, one by which a saddle is fastened upon the back of a horse.

The measure around any object, such as a body at the waist or belly, or a box ; the circumference of anything; as, in order to be acceptable for mailing, the total of height and girth of a package must not exceed 63 inches.
quotes: He's a lusty, jolly fellow, that lives well, at least three yards in the girth.

defs: A small horizontal brace or girder.

records.ety: [From Girth, n., cf. Girt, v. t.]

defs: To bind as with a girth.
